profile.name: Sean J. Westwood
profile.title: Associate Professor, Department of Government
bio: Sean is an Associate Professor in the Department of Government at Dartmouth College and director of the Polarization Research Lab (PRL). His primary area of research is political behavior and public opinion. His work focuses on understanding where partisan biases originate, where they manifest (inside and outside political domains), and their bounds. He is particularly interested at examining how partisanship and information from political elites affect the behavior of citizens. He has expertise in both survey methods and computational social science. His work has been published in journals including Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, American Political Science Review, Nature Human Behavior, and the American Journal of Political Science.

His work has been covered by The New York Times, Washington Post, Wall Street Journal, Slate, Vox, Foreign Affairs, New Republic, Newsweek, ABC, CBS, CNN, Fortune, Financial Times, Bloomberg, NPR, New York Magazine, USA Today and other local outlets.

teachingEvaluations.course: Conservatism, Liberalism, and American Culture Wars
positiveComments:
Best professor ever! Westwood has high expectations but it made me work really hard. He clearly put a lot of time and research into each lecture, and it
definitely impacted the learning experience overall. The presentations starting in week 6 allows students to do the work Westwood had been doing for a
week on topics we were not comfortable with (topics that went against our own views), which fulfilled the goal of the course as a whole.
Explained arguments clearly
Greatest professor in the department. Not even close.
He was very good at lecturing. Professor Westwood always had an answer to a question in class

I loved the lecture and questions style, it was well balances and allowed us to really debate and discuss delicate topics, also the student presentations I
think helped me become a better presenter
Lectures
Lectures and discussions

Professor Westwood is SO knowledgeable about every topic we covered. His class was so engaging, informative, and backed by data. Great discussions
emerged from his presentations. I did not want to miss a single class. He pushed everyone to think deeper about each topic. His teaching was unbiased.
He created a safe space where everyone felt welcome to contribute to heated discussions. He welcomed every perspective. Incredible class.

Professor Westwood is the best professor I have had at Dartmouth. He is deeply educated on each topic he presents in class, clearly having put hours of
research and thought into each lecture. He handles - and asks - questions with poise and careful consideration. Furthermore, he is a great
conversationalist and listens to students concerns as a helpful and honest resource. A politically open minded professor is hard to come by, but Professor
Westwood has accomplished this extraordinarily well.

COURSE ASSESSMENT REPORTS - Student Initiated Comments

This class is obviously unlike any other at Dartmouth, and I think it's needed. Westwood was an EXCELLENT lecturer. He has a way of using a storytelling
style unlike I've ever seen before and I felt so fortunate to learn from him every day. Additionally, the amount of data he presented on every topic was
incredible and I think this style very much improved my understanding and ability to craft an objective argument.

kept his own opinions removed, focusing on the side he represented. very unemotional, especially for some of the more contentious issues; completely
fact and research-based. did not really weigh in on student opinions; allowed them to be shared without validating or undermining them -- this created an
environment where anyone could share without feeling like they were judged or had to say what the prof wanted to hear.

Everything mentioned above. Westwood is a fabulous teacher, and you can't even get annoyed with him when the concepts are difficult because he's
clearly so knowledgable and explains them so well. He should continue with the overall structure of the class and homework.
Incredibly charismatic and knowledgeable lecturer.
Integrating 6 7 into the slides LOL In general very engaging / helped explain statistical concepts to everyone in room with banter
Professor Westwood is very engaging and cares to get to know his students. It doesn't go unnoticed and is appreciated.

Professor Westwood was very available to meet outside of class if we had questions. He seemed individually invested in all of our learning experiences,
which I really appreciated. He also was sure to make lecture engaging, encouraged questions and participation, and was eager to have interesting
discussions.

Professor Westwood was very kind and helpful, always willing to answer questions during class and in office hours. He was very fair. The examples he
used in class were interesting and helped me understand complex concepts. Professor Westwood was very personable and great at making students feel
comfortable in class and in office hours. He's funny. I honestly can't glaze him enough. Thank you Prof Westwood.
nothing, best proof ever!!
- Explain concepts in lecture very clearly
Being there for students but challenging them in a very respectful way. Lectures are also great and easy to understand

I think prof Westwood is one of the best ive had at Dartmouth and this is due to many things including his dynamic teaching style. his enthusiasm and
energy made the class engaging and helped maintain student interest. Including the thoughtful selection of readings and materials, and the variety of
perspectives and case studies enriched the course. As well as the inclusive classroom atmosphere, he encouraged respectful dialogue and made it easy
for students with different viewpoints to participate.
Kept a light, casual, intellectually-curious atmosphere. Really enjoyable to be in class. Also explained things clearly.
Literally the best professor I have had during my four years here as a government major
the professor was hilarious and made the classroom vibes really positive

I enjoyed the structure of the class, especially the emphasis on self-driven simulations that placed us within the context of realistic scenarios. I also
enjoyed the communal nature of the class--it made it a lot easier to learn from one another as we chipped in our commentary.
I liked turning the 3a into one class and then having the x hour. That long block period made learning easier.

The professor did an excellent job introducing many relevant examples in order to defend his assertions in class, which I appreciated. His humor and
interesting comparisons helped keep lectures engaging. He took full advantage of the small class size to foster a more conversational atmosphere; he was
however still fully able to command respect.
